free_text: Production of Fe-52 via proton-induced reactions
on manganese and nickel
Separated-sector Cyclotron Facility
Cross-sections and radionuclide yields were calculated
from the measured emission rates of the characteristic
gamma-rays of the relevant activation products, making
use of the decay data tables of Reus et al. (1983).
For irradiation, the foil stacks were mounted either
in one of the standard Faraday cups used for beam
diagnostic purposes in the NAC external beam lines,
or in a simple, more easily accessible stack holder
at the end of one of the beam lines.
All yields were corrected for decay losses during and
after bombardment, and are therefore reported as
(instantaneous) 'production rates', rather
than 'yields'.
Corrections for the decrease in beam current due to
nonelastic nuclear interactions as the beam traversed
the stacks were taken from the tabulation of
Janni (1982) while the energy degradation in the stacks
was computed by means of the stopping power expressions
of Anderson and Ziegler (1977).
see CORRECTION
see CORRECTION
see METHOD
Total uncertainty includes partial
uncertainties described in Subents 002-009.
/By authors/ The total uncertainties in the cross-
sections and production rates were obtained by
summing all the contributing uncertainties in
quadrature and were expressed with a 1 standard
deviation (1 sigma) criterion.
SD: Updated to new date format, lower case.
Corrections according to last EXFOR rules and Dict.
BIB was updated in all Subents.

ref.J,AND,29,194... in REL-REF corrected (p.193).
SD: REACTION code was updated according to
MEMO CP-D/933rev. in Subents 003-004.
SD: Corrections in Subents 006,007.
SD: Corrections in Subents 005,006.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
SD: Correction in Subent 008.
Each of the target foils was followed by a 250 mu-m
high-purity Al catcher foil (99.999%).
These foils also served as monitor foils for the
accurate measurement of the incident proton flux.
Additional beam degradation within the stacks, if
required, was accomplished by means of suitable Al
foils and/or Cu foils or discs. All the foils and discs
had a diameter of 19 mm and their individual
thicknesses were determined by weighing.
The monitor reaction cross-sections were based
on accurate Faraday-cup measurements.

From Dr. G.F.Steyn (2014-09-02): The Ni(p,x)55Co yield
used for the correction (REATION (1)) is from the
measurement on the same set of Ni targets, but it is
unpublished and no longer available.
From Dr. N.Otsuka (24.09.2024): It actually gives
Ni(p,x)55Co decay contribution used for subtraction of
the decay contribution to obtain the 008.2 dataset.
